nodes.context: North American neo-Nazi organization adhering to Christian Identity, originally based in Idaho.
Christian Identity church founded by Wesley A. Swift, later controlled by Richard Butler and linked to Aryan Nations.
Black religious and political organization led by Louis Farrakhan, mentioned as a black ally of Aryan Nations.
White supremacist terrorist group formed by Robert Jay Mathews and Aryan Nations members, active 1983-1984.
Group formed by Dennis McGiffen, inspired by The Order, with ties to Aryan Nations.
Splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Jay Faber, based in New York and later Texas.
Splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Paul Mullet, with branches in 17 states.

nodes.standard_name: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
nodes.original_text_name: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
nodes.context: Enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations according to the government.
Islamist terrorist group that August Kreis III attempted to form an alliance with.

edges.context: Aryan Nations was founded as the paramilitary wing of the Church of Jesus Christ–Christian.
Richard Butler called Louis Farrakhan a black ally in the plan to divide the US into racial mini-states.
The Order was formed by Robert Jay Mathews and Aryan Nations members, sharing ideology and members.
Dennis McGiffen, who had ties to Aryan Nations, formed The New Order inspired by The Order.
Aryan Nations Revival was a splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Jay Faber.
Crusaders of Yahweh was a splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Paul Mullet.

edges.target: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
edges.context: The government concluded that the Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood was the enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations.
August Kreis III attempted to form an Aryan Nations–al Qaeda alliance in 2005.
